ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sensor */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of sensor (e.g., magnetometer, accelerometer) */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the sensor */,
  "model_number" TEXT /* Model number of the sensor */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the sensor */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was last calibrated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parameters" (
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor the parameter belongs to */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter (e.g., Dynamic Range, Linearity) */,
  "symbol" TEXT /* Symbol for the parameter (e.g., MDR_XY, LIN) */,
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* ID of the condition under which the parameter is measured */,
  "parameter_type" TEXT /* Type of parameter (e.g., dynamic, static) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_parameters_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_parameters_parameter_type FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_type") REFERENCES parameter_types ("type_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_parameters_condition_id FOREIGN KEY ("condition_id") REFERENCES conditions ("condition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_specs" (
  "spec_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance specification */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parameter the specification belongs to */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum value of the parameter */,
  "typ_value" REAL /* Typical value of the parameter */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum value of the parameter */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the unit of measurement for the parameter */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number of the performance specification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("spec_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_specs_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES parameters ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_specs_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES units ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "datasheets" (
  "datasheet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each datasheet */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor the datasheet belongs to */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the datasheet */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the datasheet in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the datasheet was uploaded */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the datasheet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("datasheet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_datasheets_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, quality control specialist, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the sensor */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the sensor was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "units" (
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each unit */,
  "unit_name" TEXT /* Name of the unit (e.g., uT, %FS, uT/LSB) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the unit */,
  "unit_type" TEXT /* Type of unit (e.g., magnetic, acceleration) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_units_unit_type FOREIGN KEY ("unit_type") REFERENCES unit_types ("type_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "conditions" (
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each condition */,
  "condition_name" TEXT /* Name of the condition (e.g., TA=25 oC, X-axis) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the condition */,
  "condition_type" TEXT /* Type of condition (e.g., temperature, axis) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_conditions_condition_type FOREIGN KEY ("condition_type") REFERENCES condition_types ("type_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "parameter_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter type (e.g., dynamic, static) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the parameter type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "unit_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each unit type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the unit type (e.g., magnetic, acceleration) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the unit type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "condition_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each condition type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the condition type (e.g., temperature, axis) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the condition type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)
